name: Harvest Vegetable Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 28 oz. cans whole tomatoes in tomato juice
3 10 oz. bags washed ready to eat salad spinach OR
3 10 oz. pkgs. frozen chopped spinach -- thawed
1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper, divided
4 eggs (or Egg Beaters or substitute), beaten
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large colander or sieve set over a bowl, drain the tomatoes. Save the juice, covered and refrigerated, for later use.
2. Cut the tomatoes in half, placing the cut side down in the colander to drain well. Squeeze out seeds from each half.
3. Place tomatoes in a lightly oiled 12-inch nonstick skillet. Saute over high heat, stirring until all juices have evaporated, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook until soft, about 3 minutes.
4. Add tomato paste; stir gently to combine. Cook until no juice remains, about 5 minutes. Turn the mixture onto a large plate lined with paper towels to cool.
5. Cook spinach in batches with 1 cup water in the skillet, covered, over high heat until wilted, about 5 minutes. Remove to colander to drain and cool.
6. Squeeze the spinach by handfuls until very dry (if using frozen chopped spinach, skip this step and squeeze out moisture).
7. Chop the remaining spinach finely with a knife on a cutting board. Return to the colander to drain again. Squeeze once more.
8. In a medium bowl, mix spinach with nutmeg, 1/2 tsp. salt, and 1/2 tsp. pepper. Place on paper towel-lined plate.
9. Trim woody ends from mushroom stems. Shred mushrooms in batches using a shredding blade in a food processor (or mince by hand). In a lightly oiled skillet over high heat, saute until all moisture evaporates, up to 20 minutes. Season with remaining salt and pepper. Turn onto paper towel-lined plate to cool.
10. In the same skillet, saute onions with sugar until caramelized but not burned, about 20 minutes. Turn out onto a paper towel-lined plate to cool.
11. At this point, you may divide the filling ingredients into separate resealable plastic bags with folded paper towels and refrigerate.
12. To assemble the pie: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
13. In a medium bowl, mix feta cheese, beaten eggs, and spinach mixture.
14. Unfold phyllo sheets flat on waxed paper. Cover top completely with a clean damp towel. Lightly oil a 9 to 10-inch round springform pan.
15. Remove one sheet of phyllo and cover remaining sheets with a damp towel. Working toward the center, spray or brush a sheet lightly with olive oil or butter-flavored cooking spray. Ease it onto the bottom of the pan. Pat dough against sides; let excess hang over the pan edge.
16. Repeat with the second sheet at an angle to the first, and repeat with the third and fourth sheets at angles so all four quarters of the pan are covered.
17. Layer 4 additional oiled sheets. Oil both sides of another phyllo sheet, fold in half, and ease into the bottom of the pan for support.
18. Add fillings layer by layer: spinach-feta-egg mixture, mushrooms, 1/2 cup mozzarella, tomatoes, 1/2 cup mozzarella, onions.
19. Bring phyllo sheets up over the top and lightly oil. Place another oiled sheet on top. Cut into fourths, folding each piece in half lengthwise. Roll up loosely in a jelly-roll fashion, pinching at the bottom to attach petals.
20. Place the springform pan on a baking sheet to catch drips. Bake in the center of the oven until phyllo is golden brown and filling is cooked through, about 1 hour.
21. Remove from oven and let sit for 10-15 minutes. Loosen phyllo with a thin spatula from the pan. Unclasp the springform pan and remove sides. Lift pie onto a serving plate.
22. Decorate the sides of the plate as desired with parsley, flowers, leaves, etc.
